qa_pairs.question: What are the peer review committees available for Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: RAC applications can be reviewed in one of the following committees: Astronomy, Astrophysics and Cosmology; Bioinformatics; Chemistry, Biochemistry and Biophysics; Computer Sciences and Mathematics; Engineering; Environmental and Earth Sciences; Humanities and Social Sciences; Nano, Materials and Condensed Matter; Neurosciences, Medical Imaging and Medical Physics; and Subatomic Physics, Nuclear Physics and Space Physics.

qa_pairs.question: What are the primary evaluation criteria for Resources for Research Groups (RRG) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Resources for Research Groups (RRG) applications are evaluated against two main criteria: 'Research Methods', which accounts for 70% of the score, and 'Resource Management and Computational Expertise', which accounts for 30% of the score.

qa_pairs.question: How are resource allocation requests scaled annually in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Resource allocation requests are scaled every year based on the overall score of the application, as well as the supply and demand for resources. A specific scaling function is applied to compute requests to facilitate allocation decisions when capacity is insufficient.
